1: """CUBYZ_FOLDING_TUI.py -- Textual-based client rewrite.
2: 
3: Mirrors all the crunching/submission/benchmark logic from CUBYZ_FOLDING.py exactly,
4: but replaces its raw-terminal UI (print_terminal_status / DualStatusBoard / pause-menu
5: input() calls) with a Textual App that has:
6: 
7:   Left sidebar  – control buttons (same actions as the old pause-menu, always visible)
8:   Top bar       – campaign mode badge + volunteer name + global progress + ETA
9:   Lanes panel   – one box per active lane (GPU / CPU / P1 / P2 ...)
10:   Terminal log  – scrolling install/startup output + a text-input for prompts
11: 
12: All crunching runs in background threads exactly as before; the TUI is a separate
13: asyncio event-loop in the main thread (run_sync mode).  Thread->TUI communication
14: uses a thread-safe deque for log lines and a simple shared dict for lane states;
15: the TUI polls both every 0.5 s with set_interval.
16: 
17: Run:
18:     python3 CUBYZ_FOLDING_TUI.py
19: 
20: Requires textual and rich:
21:     pip install textual rich
22: """
23: 
24: from __future__ import annotations
25:

43: 
44: # -- Textual / Rich ------------------------------------------------------------

47: from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal
48: from textual.widgets import Static, RichLog, Button, Input, Label
49: 
50: # -- Platform detection --------------------------------------------------------
51: if sys.platform.startswith("linux"):
52:     PLATFORM = "linux"
53: elif sys.platform == "darwin":
54:     PLATFORM = "darwin"
55: elif sys.platform.startswith("win32"):
56:     PLATFORM = "win32"
57: else:
58:     print(f"[X] Unsupported operating system: {sys.platform}")
59:     sys.exit(1)
60: 
61: if PLATFORM == "win32":
62:     os.system('color')
63: 
64: # Always enable color codes -- Textual's RichLog interprets them via Text.from_ansi
65: class Colors:
66:     RESET   = "\033[0m"
67:     BOLD    = "\033[1m"
68:     DIM     = "\033[2m"
69:     RED     = "\033[91m"
70:     GREEN   = "\033[92m"
71:     YELLOW  = "\033[93m"
72:     BLUE    = "\033[94m"
73:     MAGENTA = "\033[95m"
74:     CYAN    = "\033[96m"
75:     GRAY    = "\033[90m"
76:     WHITE   = "\033[97m"
77: 
78: MODE_COLORS = {
79:     "rag":      Colors.CYAN,
80:     "finetune": Colors.MAGENTA,
81:     "audit":    Colors.BLUE,
82:     "idle":     Colors.GRAY,
83: }
84: MODE_LABELS = {
85:     "rag":      "RAG",
86:     "finetune": "FINETUNE",
87:     "audit":    "AUDIT",
88:     "idle":     "IDLE",
89: }
90: 
91: def mode_color(mode: str) -> str:
92:     return MODE_COLORS.get(mode, Colors.BLUE)
93: 
94: # -- Server / client constants -------------------------------------------------
95: SERVER_URL       = "http://ashframe.net:7000"
96: OLLAMA_URL       = "http://localhost:11434/api/generate"
97: CONFIG_FILE      = os.path.expanduser("~/.cubyz_node_config.json")
98: DIAGNOSTICS_FILE = os.path.expanduser("~/.cubyz_node_diagnostics.jsonl")
99: VERSION          = "1.2.7"
100: 
101: print_lock = threading.Lock()
102: 
103: # -- Shared TUI state (written by background threads, read by TUI) -------------
104: # Lane state: { lane_tag: {"label": str, "task": str, "status": str, "speed": str} }
105: _lane_state: dict = {}
106: _lane_order: list = []
107: _lane_lock  = threading.Lock()
108: 
109: # Global progress (from last /get_work response)
110: _global_progress = {"comp": 0, "tot": 0, "eta": None, "mode": "idle"}
111: _progress_lock = threading.Lock()
112: 
113: # Log deque -- background threads push lines here; TUI drains it every poll
114: _log_queue: deque = deque(maxlen=2000)
115: 
116: # Input queue -- TUI pushes user input here; background threads block on it
117: _input_queue: queue.Queue = queue.Queue()
118: 
119: # Current volunteer info (set from main thread, read by TUI)
120: _volunteer_id: str = ""
121: _dual_controller_ref     = None
122: _parallel_controller_ref = None
123: _has_gpu: bool = False
124: 
125: 
126: def _tui_log(msg: str):
127:     _log_queue.append(msg)
128: 
129: 
130: def _tui_input(prompt: str = "") -> str:
131:     _tui_log(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}{prompt}{Colors.RESET}")
132:     return _input_queue.get()
133: 
134: 
135: # Monkey-patch builtins so legacy code uses TUI I/O
136: _orig_print = builtins.print
137: _orig_input = builtins.input
138: 
139: 
140: def _patched_print(*args, **kwargs):
141:     sep  = kwargs.get("sep", " ")
142:     end  = kwargs.get("end", "\n")
143:     line = sep.join(str(a) for a in args) + end
144:     _log_queue.append(line.rstrip("\n"))
145: 
146: 
147: def _patched_input(prompt: str = "") -> str:
148:     return _tui_input(prompt)
149: 
150: 
151: builtins.print = _patched_print
152: builtins.input  = _patched_input
153:

155: # -- Version helpers -----------------------------------------------------------
156: def _parse_version(v: str) -> tuple:
157:     try:
158:         return tuple(int(p) for p in v.strip().split("."))
159:     except Exception:
160:         return (0,)

225: USER_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-zA-Z]+$")

228: def is_valid_user_id(user_id: str) -> bool:
229:     return bool(user_id) and 3 <= len(user_id) <= 12 and bool(USER_ID_PATTERN.fullmatch(user_id))

232: # -- HTTP helper ---------------------------------------------------------------
233: def make_request(url: str, data: dict = None, timeout: int = 30) -> dict:
234:     headers  = {"Content-Type": "application/json", "User-Agent": f"CubyzClient/{VERSION}"}
235:     req_data = json.dumps(data).encode("utf-8") if data is not None else None
236:     req      = urllib.request.Request(url, data=req_data, headers=headers,
237:                                        method="POST" if req_data else "GET")
238:     with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
239:         return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
240:

243: def log_diagnostic(event: dict):
244:     event["timestamp"] = time.time()
245:     try:
246:         with open(DIAGNOSTICS_FILE, "a") as f:
247:             f.write(json.dumps(event) + "\n")
248:     except Exception:
249:         pass
250: 
251: 
252: def submit_diagnostic_to_server(event: dict):
253:     try:
254:         make_request(f"{SERVER_URL}/diagnostics", event, timeout=10)
255:     except Exception:
256:         pass

259: # -- Format helpers ------------------------------------------------------------
260: def format_count(n) -> str:
261:     try:
262:         return f"{int(n):,}"
263:     except Exception:
264:         return str(n)
265: 
266: 
267: def format_eta(seconds) -> str:
268:     if seconds is None or seconds < 0:
269:         return "calculating..."
270:     seconds = int(seconds)
271:     if seconds < 60:
272:         return f"{seconds}s"
273:     days, rem = divmod(seconds, 86400)
274:     hours, rem = divmod(rem, 3600)
275:     minutes, _ = divmod(rem, 60)
276:     if days:
277:         return f"{days}d {hours}h"
278:     if hours:
279:         return f"{hours}h {minutes}m"
280:     return f"{minutes}m"
281: 
282: 
283: # -- Update logic --------------------------------------------------------------
284: def _https_context() -> ssl.SSLContext:
285:     try:
286:         import certifi
287:         return ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
288:     except ImportError:
289:         pass
290:     for candidate in ("/etc/ssl/cert.pem", "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"):
291:         if os.path.exists(candidate):
292:             return ssl.create_default_context(cafile=candidate)
293:     return ssl.create_default_context()
294: 
295: 
296: def check_for_update():
297:     try:
298:         info = make_request(f"{SERVER_URL}/version", timeout=10)
299:     except Exception:
300:         return "check_failed", None
301:     min_v    = info.get("min_client_version", "0.0.0")
302:     latest_v = info.get("latest_client_version", VERSION)
303:     if _parse_version(VERSION) < _parse_version(min_v):
304:         return "must_update", info
305:     if _parse_version(VERSION) < _parse_version(latest_v):
306:         return "update_available", info
307:     return "current", info
308: 
309: 
310: def download_update(download_url: str, expected_version: str) -> bool:
311:     try:
312:         with urllib.request.urlopen(download_url, timeout=30, context=_https_context()) as res:
313:             new_content = res.read()
314:     except Exception as e:
315:         print(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Update download failed: {e}{Colors.RESET}")
316:         return False
317:     match = re.search(rb'^VERSION\s*=\s*["\']([\\d.]+)["\']', new_content, re.MULTILINE)
318:     downloaded_version = match.group(1).decode() if match else None
319:     if downloaded_version != expected_version:
320:         print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}[!] Downloaded v{downloaded_version or '?'} != expected v{expected_version}. Skipping.{Colors.RESET}")
321:         return False
322:     this_file = os.path.abspath(__file__)
323:     try:
324:         with open(this_file, "wb") as f:
325:             f.write(new_content)
326:     except Exception as e:
327:         print(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Could not write update: {e}{Colors.RESET}")
328:         return False
329:     print(f"{Colors.GREEN}[OK] Update downloaded.{Colors.RESET}")
330:     return True
331: 
332: 
333: def offer_update(status: str, info: dict, mandatory: bool):
334:     latest_v     = info.get("latest_client_version", "?")
335:     download_url = info.get("download_url", "")
336:     if mandatory:
337:         print(f"\n{Colors.RED}{Colors.BOLD}[X] Client v{VERSION} no longer accepted -- update required.{Colors.RESET}")
338:     else:
339:         print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}[i] Update available: v{latest_v} (you have v{VERSION}).{Colors.RESET}")
340:     if load_auto_update_preference():
341:         print(f"    {Colors.CYAN}[~] Auto-update enabled -- downloading...{Colors.RESET}")
342:         choice = 'y'
343:     else:
344:         choice = input(f"    {Colors.YELLOW}Download the update now? (y/n): {Colors.RESET}").strip().lower()
345:     if choice != 'y':
346:         if mandatory:
347:             sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Cannot continue on an unsupported version.{Colors.RESET}")
348:         return
349:     if download_update(download_url, latest_v):
350:         print(f"{Colors.GREEN}[OK] Restarting...{Colors.RESET}")
351:         os.execv(sys.executable, [sys.executable] + sys.argv)
352:     elif mandatory:
353:         sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Could not auto-update. Update manually: {download_url}{Colors.RESET}")
354: 
355: 
356: # -- Hardware detection --------------------------------------------------------
357: def get_linux_distro() -> str:
358:     try:
359:         with open("/etc/os-release", "r") as f:
360:             for line in f:
361:                 if line.startswith("ID="):
362:                     return line.strip().split("=")[1].strip('"')
363:     except Exception:
364:         pass
365:     return "unknown"
366: 
367: 
368: def get_system_ram_gb() -> float:
369:     if PLATFORM == "darwin":
370:         try:
371:             out = subprocess.run(["sysctl", "-n", "hw.memsize"], capture_output=True, text=True, check=True).stdout.strip()
372:             return int(out) / (1024.0 ** 3)
373:         except Exception:
374:             return 8.0
375:     if PLATFORM == "win32":
376:         try:
377:             result = subprocess.run(["wmic", "ComputerSystem", "get", "TotalPhysicalMemory", "/value"],
378:                                     capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15)
379:             for line in result.stdout.split("\n"):
380:                 if "TotalPhysicalMemory" in line and "=" in line:
381:                     return int(line.split("=")[1].strip()) / (1024.0 ** 3)
382:         except Exception:
383:             pass
384:         return 8.0
385:     try:
386:         with open("/proc/meminfo", "r") as f:
387:             for line in f:
388:                 if line.startswith("MemTotal:"):
389:                     return int(line.split()[1]) / (1024.0 * 1024.0)
390:     except Exception:
391:         pass
392:     return 8.0
393: 
394: 
395: def is_apple_silicon() -> bool:
396:     return platform.machine() == "arm64"
397: 
398: 
399: def check_apple_silicon_gpu() -> tuple:
400:     if not is_apple_silicon():
401:         return False, 0.0
402:     ram_gb = get_system_ram_gb()
403:     return True, min(ram_gb - 3.5, ram_gb * 0.75)
404: 
405: 
406: def check_nvidia_gpu() -> tuple:
407:     try:
408:         res = subprocess.run(['nvidia-smi', '--query-gpu=memory.total', '--format=csv,nounits,noheader'],
409:                              capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, timeout=15)
410:         vram = int(res.stdout.strip().split('\n')[0]) / 1024.0
411:         return True, vram
412:     except Exception:
413:         pass
414:     return False, 0.0
415: 
416: 
417: def _amd_vram_from_sysfs() -> float:
418:     try:
419:         for p in glob.glob("/sys/class/drm/card*/device/mem_info_vram_total"):
420:             with open(p) as f:
421:                 val = int(f.read().strip())
422:                 if val > 0:
423:                     return val / (1024.0 ** 3)
424:     except Exception:
425:         pass
426:     return 0.0
427: 
428: 
429: def check_amd_gpu() -> tuple:
430:     vram = _amd_vram_from_sysfs()
431:     if vram > 0:
432:         return True, vram
433:     for tool in ["rocm-smi", "/opt/rocm/bin/rocm-smi"]:
434:         try:
435:             res  = subprocess.run([tool, "--showmeminfo", "vram", "--json"],
436:                                   capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
437:             data = json.loads(res.stdout)
438:             for card_data in data.values():
439:                 total = card_data.get("VRAM Total Memory (B)", 0)
440:                 if total:
441:                     return True, int(total) / (1024.0 ** 3)
442:         except Exception:
443:             pass
444:     try:
445:         pci = subprocess.run(['lspci'], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout
446:         if "amd" in pci.lower() or "radeon" in pci.lower():
447:             return True, vram
448:     except Exception:
449:         pass
450:     return False, 0.0
451: 
452: 
453: def check_intel_gpu() -> tuple:
454:     try:
455:         pci = subprocess.run(['lspci'], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout
456:         if "intel" in pci.lower() and ("vga" in pci.lower() or "display" in pci.lower()):
457:             return True, 0.0
458:     except Exception:
459:         pass
460:     return False, 0.0
461: 
462: 
463: # -- GPU tier / model selection ------------------------------------------------
464: GPU_TIER_VRAM_FLOOR_GB = {"easy": 3.0, "medium": 6.0, "hard": 12.0}
465: CPU_TIER_RAM_FLOOR_GB  = {"easy": 6.0, "medium": 8.0, "hard": 16.0}
466: DUAL_LANE_MIN_RAM_GB   = 16.0
467: DUAL_LANE_MIN_VRAM_GB  = 3.0
468: TIER_RANK              = {"easy": 0, "medium": 1, "hard": 2}
469: BENCHMARK_VERSION      = "4"
470: BENCHMARK_TIMEOUT      = 90
471: PARALLEL_WORKERS_BY_TIER                           = {"easy": 2, "medium": 2, "hard": 3}
472: PARALLEL_WORKERS_MAX                               = max(PARALLEL_WORKERS_BY_TIER.values())
473: PARALLEL_WORKERS_VRAM_HEADROOM_PER_WORKER_GB       = {"easy": 1.5, "medium": 2.5, "hard": 4.0}
474: PARALLEL_WORKERS_RAM_HEADROOM_PER_WORKER_GB        = {"easy": 2.0, "medium": 3.0, "hard": 5.0}
475: 
476: 
477: def gpu_tier_from_vram(vram: float) -> str:
478:     if vram <= 4.5:
479:         return "easy"
480:     if vram <= 8.5:
481:         return "medium"
482:     return "hard"
483: 
484: 
485: def get_cpu_model_tier(ram_gb: float):
486:     if ram_gb >= 8.0:
487:         return "qwen2.5-coder:7b", "medium"
488:     if ram_gb >= 6.0:
489:         return "qwen2.5-coder:3b", "easy"
490:     return None, None
491: 
492: 
493: def get_vram_and_choose_model() -> tuple:
494:     if PLATFORM == "darwin":
495:         ok, vram = check_apple_silicon_gpu()
496:         if ok:
497:             if vram >= 20.0: return "qwen2.5-coder:32b", "apple_silicon", vram
498:             if vram >= 12.0: return "qwen2.5-coder:14b", "apple_silicon", vram
499:             if vram >= 6.0:  return "qwen2.5-coder:7b",  "apple_silicon", vram
500:             return "qwen2.5-coder:3b", "apple_silicon", vram
501:         ok, vram = check_intel_gpu()
502:         if ok:
503:             return "qwen2.5-coder:3b", "intel_dgpu", vram
504:     ok, vram = check_nvidia_gpu()
505:     if ok:
506:         if vram >= 20.0: return "qwen2.5-coder:32b", "nvidia", vram
507:         if vram >= 12.0: return "qwen2.5-coder:14b", "nvidia", vram
508:         if vram >= 6.0:  return "qwen2.5-coder:7b",  "nvidia", vram
509:         return "qwen2.5-coder:3b", "nvidia", vram
510:     ok, vram = check_amd_gpu()
511:     if ok:
512:         if vram >= 20.0: return "qwen2.5-coder:32b", "amd", vram
513:         if vram >= 12.0: return "qwen2.5-coder:14b", "amd", vram
514:         if vram >= 6.0:  return "qwen2.5-coder:7b",  "amd", vram
515:         return "qwen2.5-coder:3b", "amd", vram
516:     ram_gb = get_system_ram_gb()
517:     cpu_model, cpu_tier = get_cpu_model_tier(ram_gb)
518:     if cpu_model is None:
519:         sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Insufficient RAM ({ram_gb:.1f} GB). Min 6 GB required.{Colors.RESET}")
520:     return cpu_model, "cpu", 0.0
521: 
522: 
523: # -- Benchmark -----------------------------------------------------------------
524: BENCHMARK_PROMPT = "Write a single-sentence explanation of what a hash map is."
525: 
526: 
527: def _ollama_generate(model: str, prompt: str, force_cpu: bool = False, timeout: int = BENCHMARK_TIMEOUT) -> str:
528:     payload = {
529:         "model": model, "prompt": prompt, "stream": False,
530:         "options": {"num_gpu": 0 if force_cpu else 99}
531:     }
532:     req = urllib.request.Request(
533:         OLLAMA_URL,
534:         data=json.dumps(payload).encode(),
535:         headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
536:         method="POST",
537:     )
538:     with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
539:         return json.loads(resp.read().decode())["response"]
540: 
541: 
542: def benchmark_lane(model: str, force_cpu: bool = False) -> tuple:
543:     try:
544:         start = time.time()
545:         _ollama_generate(model, BENCHMARK_PROMPT, force_cpu=force_cpu, timeout=BENCHMARK_TIMEOUT)
546:         return time.time() - start, None
547:     except Exception as e:
548:         return None, str(e)[:200]
549: 
550: 
551: # -- Ollama install / run ------------------------------------------------------
552: def install_docker():
553:     distro = get_linux_distro()
554:     try:
555:         if distro in ["ubuntu", "debian", "pop", "mint"]:
556:             subprocess.run("sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io", shell=True, check=True)
557:         elif distro in ["arch", "manjaro"]:
558:             subprocess.run("sudo pacman -S --noconfirm docker", shell=True, check=True)
559:         else:
560:             subprocess.run("curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh", shell=True, check=True)
561:         subprocess.run("sudo systemctl enable --now docker", shell=True, check=True)
562:     except KeyboardInterrupt:
563:         sys.exit(f"\n{Colors.CYAN}[X] Docker install cancelled.{Colors.RESET}")
564:     except Exception as e:
565:         sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Docker install failed: {e}{Colors.RESET}")
566: 
567: 
568: def install_ollama_container(gpu_type: str):
569:     gpu_flags, image_name = "", "ollama/ollama"
570:     if gpu_type == "amd":
571:         gpu_flags, image_name = "--device /dev/kfd --device /dev/dri", "ollama/ollama:rocm"
572:     elif gpu_type == "nvidia":
573:         gpu_flags = "--gpus all"
574:     try:
575:         subprocess.run(
576:             f"sudo docker run -d -e OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL={PARALLEL_WORKERS_MAX} {gpu_flags} "
577:             f"-v ollama:/root/.ollama -p 11434:11434 --name ollama {image_name}",
578:             shell=True, check=True,
579:         )
580:     except Exception as e:
581:         sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Container deploy failed: {e}{Colors.RESET}")
582: 
583: 
584: def ensure_ollama_installed(gpu_type: str = None):
585:     if PLATFORM == "linux":
586:         has_docker = bool(shutil.which("docker"))
587:         if shutil.which("ollama") or (has_docker and "ollama" in subprocess.run(
588:                 ["docker", "ps", "-a", "--format", "{{.Names}}"], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout):
589:             return
590:         print(f"{Colors.CYAN}[~] Ollama not found. Initializing setup...{Colors.RESET}")
591:         if input("Run Ollama inside Docker? (y/n): ").strip().lower() == 'y':
592:             if not has_docker:
593:                 install_docker()
594:             install_ollama_container(gpu_type)
595:         else:
596:             try:
597:                 subprocess.run("curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh", shell=True, check=True)
598:             except Exception as e:
599:                 sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Install failed: {e}{Colors.RESET}")
600:         return
601:     if shutil.which("ollama"):
602:         return
603:     if PLATFORM == "darwin":
604:         if shutil.which("brew"):
605:             try:
606:                 subprocess.run(["brew", "install", "ollama"], check=True)
607:                 return
608:             except Exception:
609:                 pass
610:         sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Install Ollama from https://ollama.com/download/mac{Colors.RESET}")
611:     if PLATFORM == "win32":
612:         print(f"{Colors.CYAN}[~] Install Ollama from: https://ollama.com/download/windows{Colors.RESET}")
613:         input("[?] Once installed, press [ENTER] to continue... ")
614:         if not shutil.which("ollama"):
615:             sys.exit("[X] Ollama still not found. Restart terminal and try again.")
616: 
617: 
618: def _is_ollama_running() -> bool:
619:     try:
620:         urllib.request.urlopen("http://localhost:11434", timeout=3)
621:         return True
622:     except Exception:
623:         return False
624: 
625: 
626: def _start_ollama_native():
627:     try:
628:         subprocess.Popen(["ollama", "serve"], stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
629:     except Exception:
630:         pass
631: 
632: 
633: def ensure_ollama_running_and_model_pulled(model_name: str):
634:     if not _is_ollama_running():
635:         print(f"{Colors.CYAN}[~] Starting Ollama...{Colors.RESET}")
636:         _start_ollama_native()
637:         for _ in range(30):
638:             time.sleep(1)
639:             if _is_ollama_running():
640:                 break
641:         else:
642:             sys.exit(f"{Colors.RED}[X] Ollama failed to start. Start it manually and re-run.{Colors.RESET}")
643:     try:
644:         result = subprocess.run(["ollama", "list"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15)
645:         if model_name not in result.stdout:
646:             print(f"{Colors.CYAN}[~] Pulling model {model_name} (one-time download)...{Colors.RESET}")
647:             subprocess.run(["ollama", "pull", model_name], timeout=600)
648:     except Exception as e:
649:         print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}[!] Could not verify model: {e}{Colors.RESET}")
650: 
651: 
652: # -- GPU architecture check ----------------------------------------------------
653: GPU_ARCH_COMPATIBILITY = {"amd": {"unsupported": ["gfx803","gfx900","gfx902","gfx906","gfx908"]}}
654: 
655: 
656: def check_gpu_architecture_support(gpu_type: str) -> tuple:
657:     if gpu_type != "amd":
658:         return False, ""
659:     unsupported = GPU_ARCH_COMPATIBILITY.get("amd", {}).get("unsupported", [])
660:     try:
661:         for tool in ["rocminfo", "/opt/rocm/bin/rocminfo"]:
662:             if shutil.which(tool) or os.path.exists(tool):
663:                 res = subprocess.run([tool], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15)
664:                 for arch in unsupported:
665:                     if arch in res.stdout:
666:                         return True, f"GPU uses unsupported arch {arch}"
667:                 return False, ""
668:     except Exception:
669:         pass
670:     return False, ""
671: 
672: 
673: # -- Leaderboard / userlist helpers --------------------------------------------
674: def fetch_leaderboard_text() -> str:
675:     try:
676:         stats = make_request(f"{SERVER_URL}/leaderboard", timeout=10)
677:     except Exception as e:
678:         return f"{Colors.RED}[X] Could not reach server: {e}{Colors.RESET}"
679:     is_audit = stats.get("mode") == "audit"
680:     tot      = stats.get("total_chunks_in_codebase") or stats.get("total_chunks_due") or 1
681:     comp     = stats.get("total_chunks_completed", 0)
682:     pct      = stats.get("global_percentage", 0.0)
683:     bar_len  = 20
684:     filled   = max(0, min(bar_len, int(round(bar_len * comp / float(tot))))) if tot else 0
685:     p_bar    = chr(9608) * filled + chr(9617) * (bar_len - filled)
686:     lines    = [
687:         f"{Colors.YELLOW}--- [ CUBYZ LEADERBOARD ] ---{Colors.RESET}",
688:         f"  Progress : [{Colors.GREEN}{p_bar}{Colors.RESET}] {format_count(comp)}/{format_count(tot)} ({pct:.2f}%)",
689:         f"  ETA      : {format_eta(stats.get('eta_seconds'))}",
690:     ]
691:     for u in stats.get("rankings", []):
692:         rank  = u.get('rank', '?')
693:         color = f"{Colors.YELLOW}{Colors.BOLD}" if rank == 1 else ""
694:         reset = Colors.RESET if rank == 1 else ""
695:         if is_audit:
696:             stat_text = (f"{format_count(u.get('chunks_audited', 0))} audited  "
697:                          f"{format_count(u.get('issues_found', 0))} issues  "
698:                          f"{format_count(u.get('reviews_done', 0))} reviewed  "
699:                          f"{format_count(u.get('fixes_applied', 0))} fixes")
700:         else:
701:             lc        = u.get("lines_crunched", u.get("pairs_generated", 0))
702:             stat_text = f"{format_count(u.get('chunks_completed', 0))} chunks  {format_count(lc)} lines"
703:         lines.append(f"  {color}#{rank:<3}{reset} {u.get('user_id', '?'):<14} {stat_text}")
704:     lines.append(f"{Colors.YELLOW}-----------------------------{Colors.RESET}")
705:     return "\n".join(lines)
706: 
707: 
708: def fetch_userlist_text() -> str:
709:     try:
710:         stats = make_request(f"{SERVER_URL}/leaderboard", timeout=10)
711:     except Exception as e:
712:         return f"{Colors.RED}[X] Could not reach server: {e}{Colors.RESET}"
713:     users   = stats.get("rankings", [])
714:     online  = [u for u in users if "ONLINE" in u.get("status", "")]
715:     offline = [u for u in users if "ONLINE" not in u.get("status", "")]
716:     lines   = [
717:         f"{Colors.CYAN}--- [ ACTIVE NODE ROSTER ] ---{Colors.RESET}",
718:         f"  {Colors.GREEN}{len(online)} online{Colors.RESET}  {Colors.GRAY}{len(offline)} offline{Colors.RESET}",
719:     ]
720:     for u in online + offline:
721:         is_on = "ONLINE" in u.get("status", "")
722:         sc    = Colors.GREEN if is_on else Colors.GRAY
723:         lines.append(f"  {sc}{u.get('status', '?'):<10}{Colors.RESET} {u.get('user_id', '?')}")
724:     lines.append(f"{Colors.CYAN}------------------------------{Colors.RESET}")
725:     return "\n".join(lines)
726: 
727: 
728: # -- GPU diagnostic ------------------------------------------------------------
729: def run_live_gpu_diagnostic():
730:     print(f"{Colors.CYAN}[~] Running GPU diagnostic...{Colors.RESET}")
731:     ok, vram = check_nvidia_gpu()
732:     if ok:
733:         print(f"{Colors.GREEN}[OK] NVIDIA GPU detected, {vram:.1f} GB VRAM{Colors.RESET}")
734:         return
735:     ok, vram = check_amd_gpu()
736:     if ok:
737:         print(f"{Colors.GREEN}[OK] AMD GPU detected, {vram:.1f} GB VRAM{Colors.RESET}")
738:         return
739:     ok, vram = check_apple_silicon_gpu()
740:     if ok:
741:         print(f"{Colors.GREEN}[OK] Apple Silicon, {vram:.1f} GB effective{Colors.RESET}")
742:         return
743:     print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}[!] No dedicated GPU detected -- CPU-only mode{Colors.RESET}")
744:

746: # -- TUI status board (replaces DualStatusBoard) -------------------------------
747: class TuiStatusBoard:
748:     """Thread-safe board that writes into _lane_state for the TUI to render."""
749: 
750:     LANE_COLORS = ["\033[96m", "\033[95m", "\033[93m", "\033[94m", "\033[92m", "\033[91m"]
751: 
752:     def __init__(self):
753:         self._lock = threading.Lock()
754: 
755:     def set_label(self, lane_tag: str, label: str):
756:         with _lane_lock:
757:             if lane_tag not in _lane_order:
758:                 _lane_order.append(lane_tag)
759:             _lane_state.setdefault(lane_tag, {
760:                 "label": label, "task": "(waiting for a task...)",
761:                 "status": "Starting...", "speed": "calculating...",
762:             })
763:             _lane_state[lane_tag]["label"] = label
764: 
765:     def drop_lane(self, lane_tag: str):
766:         with _lane_lock:
767:             if lane_tag in _lane_order:
768:                 _lane_order.remove(lane_tag)
769:             _lane_state.pop(lane_tag, None)
770: 
771:     def should_announce_mode(self, mode: str) -> bool:
772:         return False
773: 
774:     def update(self, lane_tag: str, task_desc: str, step_msg: str,
775:                comp: int, tot: int, eta, speed: str):
776:         with _lane_lock:
777:             if lane_tag not in _lane_order:
778:                 _lane_order.append(lane_tag)
779:             _lane_state.setdefault(lane_tag, {})
780:             _lane_state[lane_tag].update({"task": task_desc, "status": step_msg, "speed": speed})
781:         with _progress_lock:
782:             _global_progress["comp"] = comp
783:             _global_progress["tot"]  = tot
784:             _global_progress["eta"]  = eta
785: 
786:     def note(self, msg: str):
787:         _log_queue.append(msg)
788: 
789:     def rendered_once(self):
790:         return True
791: 
792: 
793: # -- interruptible_sleep -------------------------------------------------------
794: def interruptible_sleep(seconds: float, dual_controller=None, parallel_controller=None, has_gpu=False):
795:     end = time.time() + seconds
796:     while time.time() < end:
797:         time.sleep(0.5)
798:
